title: Additive handlers run during Envio preload
reasoning: The reviewed handler group contains several handlers with additive writes and side effects but no `context.isPreload` early return. Other handlers in the same group explicitly document that Envio v3 invokes the same event twice for preload plus processing and guard the preload pass before RPC/effect work and writes. Broker.Swap increments daily snapshots and aggregator/trader rollups, while VirtualPool handlers call `upsertSnapshot` and leaderboard rollups. If these handlers run in the preload pass like the guarded handlers, the event-row writes may overwrite by id, but the additive snapshot counters and volumes can be incremented twice or RPC/effect work can run twice.
recommendation: Add explicit preload branches to additive handlers. In preload, warm only the entity reads/effects needed by the processing pass and return before any `set`, upsert, heartbeat, or leaderboard/snapshot mutation. Apply this at least to Broker.Swap and VirtualPool event handlers that update snapshots/rollups, then audit OpenLiquidityStrategy for the same pattern.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included broker and biPoolManager tests call `processEvent` in the normal processing path only; there is no test that invokes Broker.Swap or VirtualPool handlers with `context.isPreload === true` and then verifies no additive rollup rows are written or double counted.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add harness coverage that processes a Broker.Swap and a VirtualPool.Swap once with a preload context and once with a processing context, then asserts BrokerDailySnapshot/BrokerExchangeDailySnapshot/leaderboard snapshot counts equal one processing event, not preload plus processing.
minimumFixScope: Broker.Swap and VirtualPool handlers that perform additive rollup/snapshot writes; include a quick sibling audit of OpenLiquidityStrategy additive counters.

history.note: Broker and VirtualPool preload paths now warm reads/effects then return before additive writes; OLS preload mutation paths audited.
